demonstrates: analog VHS noise floor and snow — luma/chroma static, dropout speckle, and head-switching noise texture from a real tape with no input signal (640x480, ~6m21s, CC0 by Caleb Minear via Wikimedia Commons)

VHS artifacts over real picture content — chroma bleed, soft luma, tracking error, bottom-of-frame head-switching band, dropouts, and generational/composite loss from a 1989 Palace Video budget VHS rip of public-domain cartoons
